name: S&P Global
confidenceReason: Asset-light, unregulated duopoly with infinite pricing power.
bullCase: S&P Global's Ratings segment benefits from surging debt issuance volumes at still-elevated rates. Market Intelligence and Mobility segments are growing 10%+ organically on subscription revenue. After absorbing the IHS Markit integration, operating margins are expanding toward 50%+, driving compounding non-GAAP EPS.
bearCase: Ratings revenue is highly sensitive to debt issuance cycles — a credit freeze or prolonged rate inversion collapses new issuance and cuts Ratings revenue materially (as seen in 2022). At 19x forward PE, any deceleration below 12% EPS growth would re-rate the stock.
description: S&P Global Inc., together with its subsidiaries, provides benchmarks, data, analytics, and workflow solutions in the global capital, energy and commodity, and automotive markets.
